_note: Chapter 8 STANDS ALONE per NEC 90.3 — not subject to Chapters 1-4 unless specifically referenced. Articles 800 (general), 805 (network-powered broadband), 810 (radio/TV), 820 (cable TV), 830 (premises-powered broadband), 840 (premises-powered broadband over POE).

articles.scope: General installation requirements for ALL Chapter 8 communications systems — telephone, intercom, network, audio. Wiring/cable listing, separation from power circuits (800.133), bonding of primary protector (800.100).

key_sections.title: Cable and Primary Protector Bonding and Grounding
key_sections.summary: Primary protector grounded to building grounding electrode via #14 AWG (or larger) grounding conductor; bonded to power grounding system

key_sections.title: Installation of Communications Wires, Cables, and Equipment
key_sections.summary: Separation from power and Class 1 circuits — communications cables generally must be separated by 2 in from power conductors of >300 V unless one is in conduit

key_sections.title: Listing and Marking of Communications Wires and Cables

common_errors: Failing to bond the primary protector to the building grounding electrode (800.100) — common during retrofits
Mixing communications cables and power circuits in shared raceways without 2-in separation (800.133)
articles.divergence_notes: NEC Chapter 8 stands alone (90.3); IEC handles communications via separate standards (ISO/IEC 11801, EN 50173). The 'primary protector' concept (800.100) is NEC-specific — bonding telecom protector to the building grounding electrode.
articles.usage_notes: Chapter 8 cross-references back to Chapter 1-4 are explicit (e.g., 800.100 references Art 250). Otherwise Chapter 8 stands alone.

articles.scope: Telephone-circuit-specific requirements — primary protectors, listing of communications equipment, premises wiring.

key_sections.title: Circuit Loading
key_sections.summary: Primary protector required where communications circuit exposed to lightning or accidental contact with power circuits >300 V

common_errors: Omitting primary protector on aerial communications cable — required by 805.50
articles.divergence_notes: NEC Art 805 telephone-specific rules; IEC defers to national telecoms regulation.
articles.usage_notes: Read 805 + 800 together for the full picture.

articles.scope: Antenna systems (transmitting and receiving) — antenna lead-in cables, masts, antenna discharge units, grounding.

key_sections.title: Antenna Discharge Units
key_sections.summary: Antenna discharge unit (lightning arrester) on receiving antenna lead-ins

key_sections.title: Grounding Conductors — Receiving Stations
key_sections.summary: Antenna mast and antenna discharge unit grounded to building grounding electrode

common_errors: Omitting antenna discharge unit on rooftop receiving antenna — required by 810.20
articles.divergence_notes: Art 810 antenna grounding ≈ IEC 62305-3 (Lightning protection) external systems. NEC requires antenna discharge unit grounding for receiving antennas; IEC handles via SPD coordination.
articles.usage_notes: Always coordinate antenna grounding with the building lightning-protection system if present.

articles.article_title: Community Antenna Television and Radio Distribution Systems (CATV)
articles.scope: Cable television and coaxial distribution — same primary-protector / separation / bonding regime as Art 800.

key_sections.title: Cable and Primary Protector Bonding and Grounding
key_sections.summary: Coaxial cable shield bonded/grounded at building entrance via #14 AWG conductor

common_errors: Failing to ground the cable TV shield at building entrance
articles.divergence_notes: NEC Art 820 CATV-specific; IEC handles via ISO/IEC 11801 and national CATV regulations.
articles.usage_notes: Coordinate with the cable provider's drop installation.

articles.scope: Network-powered broadband (power from the central office over the broadband line) — primary protector requirements, separation, cable types (BMR, BLP/BLR/BL).

key_sections.title: Primary Protector Grounding and Bonding
key_sections.summary: Primary protector grounded same as Art 800

key_sections.title: Listing of Equipment, Wires, Cables, and Devices
key_sections.summary: Cable types BMR, BLP, BLR, BL by application

articles.divergence_notes: NEC Art 830 covers network-powered broadband (rare topology); IEC has no direct equivalent.
articles.usage_notes: Read in conjunction with the provider's drop equipment spec.

articles.scope: Premises-powered broadband (the customer powers the ONT/router locally) — Power over Ethernet (PoE) at the network interface, battery backup considerations.

key_sections.title: Powering Circuits
key_sections.summary: Premises power source for the broadband terminal — typically PoE (Class 2/3 power-limited)

articles.divergence_notes: NEC Art 840 covers premises-powered broadband + PoE specifics; IEC handles via IEC 60364-4-41 PELV and IEC 60950 / IEC 62368 for ONT/router equipment.
articles.usage_notes: On fiber-to-the-premises (FTTP) installations, this is the primary article — read with 770 (optical fiber).
